title: A Difference-of-Convex Functions Approach to Energy-Based Iterative Reasoning

repos.name: DanielTschernutter__DCAReasoner
repos.evidence: README states 'This repository contains code for the paper > D. Tschernutter, D. Diego-Castro, M. Kasiński (2025). A Difference-of-Convex Functions Approach to Energy-Based Iterative Reasoning. NeurIPS.' Repo owner DanielTschernutter matches lead author Daniel Tschernutter. Contains model/dca_reasoner.py, trainer/, evaluator/, data/datasets.py and main.py — all implementing the paper's DCAReasoner method.

rationale: The single cloned repo DanielTschernutter__DCAReasoner is unambiguously the paper's own implementation: the README names the exact paper title, venue (NeurIPS), and all three authors, and the repo owner matches the lead author. The code contains the core DCA-based energy reasoning algorithm (dca_reasoner.py), trainer, evaluator, and all five benchmark dataset generators described in Appendix B.3. The audit's findings (missing baselines, no timing code, absent text-classification experiment, no seeding, no multi-run harness) are all legitimate 'missing reproducibility artifacts' findings written against the correct repo. No non-core repo was cloned; the two baseline repos (yilundu/ired_code_release and yilundu/irem_code_release) were identified in code_links.txt but tagged [baseline-cue] and NOT cloned, so there is no contamination.
audit_impact: None — the audit correctly identified DanielTschernutter__DCAReasoner as the paper's own core method repo and all findings are written against it. The missing-code findings (baselines, timing, Table 2 experiment, multi-run harness) are valid and accurately describe gaps in the released code relative to the full set of reported experiments.
recommended_action: No re-audit needed. The paper is correctly classified as source-present (partial implementation). All eight findings can stand as-is.
